iocs.notes: Attacker source IP; both for HTTP recon (nginx access.log) and SSH foothold (auth.log). Same IP appears as exfiltration destination on port 8443.
Dropped attacker payload. Set immutable (chattr +i) after install.
Persistence account created post-foothold. Username is intentionally innocuous; the UID range is the giveaway.
Persistence via cron under the support user, fragmenting the forensic trail.
Exfiltration destination. Same IP as the attacker source; non-standard port 8443.
zgrab is a legitimate scanner; the IP correlation is what makes this a high-signal IOC.

iocs.value: post-sudo wget-to-/tmp followed by chmod+x and immediate execute
iocs.notes: The canonical post-foothold payload-drop sequence. Useful for cross-organisation pattern matching.

iocs.value: history -c followed by rm ~/.bash_history followed by exit, within 10 seconds
iocs.notes: Defence-evasion sequence (MITRE ATT&CK T1070.003). The timing tells you the attacker is operating from a script, not interactively.
